revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:井関三神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Iseki Sanjinja | image = File:Isekisan Shrine Shaden.jpg | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Tatsuno, Hyōgo|en|Tatsuno, Hyōgo|qid=Q877190|lt=Tatsuno City|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}} {{ill|Isai Village|ja|揖西村|lt=Isai Town|lt_ja=揖西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isai Village|1=Issai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498437}} 中垣内甲799-1 | Coordinates = Main Shrine:{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|52|33.1|N|134|31|12.9|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Iseki Sanjinja Main Shrine}}
Okumiya:{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|11.0|N|134|31|7.9|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Okumiya}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu Kuniteruhiko Hikari Kushi Tama Hoyosui no Mikoto|ja|天照国照彦火明櫛玉饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Kuniteruhiko Hikari Kushi Tama Hoyosui no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10949054}}
{{ill|Seoitsu‐hime no Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoitsu‐hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13579836}}
{{ill|Tatemina no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9150391}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Old Hometown Shrine|ja|旧郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Hometown Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}
{{ill|Former Sacred Offerings Shrine|ja|旧神饌幣帛料供進指定神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sacred Offerings Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Founded = In the 2nd year of Emperor Denshōjin (96 BC) | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare‐zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Also Known As = Iseki Sansha Daimyōjin
Shikichi Amaterasu Shrine | Offerings, etc. = | Annual Festival = October 10 | Rituals = 10‐Year Festival Revelation Ceremony
Sairen Bōzu | Map = {{Location map+|Japan Hyōgo| width=200|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=52|lat_sec=33.1|lon_deg=134|lon_min=31|lon_sec=7.9|position=top|label=Main Shrine}} {{Location map~|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=52|lat_sec=33.1|lon_deg=134|lon_min=31|lon_sec=12.9|position=bottom|label=Okumiya}}}} | location = {{ill|Tatsuno|qid=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.875861|N|134.52025|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Nigihayahi|qid=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Iseki Sanjinja Okumiya | image = [[File:Iseki okumiya04.JPG|270px|center|]] | Location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Tatsuno, Hyōgo|en|Tatsuno, Hyōgo|qid=Q877190|lt=Tatsuno City|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}} {{ill|Isai Village|ja|揖西村|lt=Isai Town|lt_ja=揖西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isai Village|1=Issai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498437}} 中垣内甲 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|11.0|N|134|31|7.9|E|region:JP}} | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Takeiwamitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiwamitsuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1502690}} | Founded = In the 2nd year of Emperor Denshōjin (96 BC) | Also Known As = Iseki Okumiya Shrine
Okumiya Taijin Shrine
Okumiya Daimyōjin }} '''Iseki Sanjinja''' (井関三神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nakagaitsu, within {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Tatsuno, Hyōgo|en|Tatsuno, Hyōgo|qid=Q877190|lt=Tatsuno City|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}} {{ill|Isai Village|ja|揖西村|lt=Isai Town|lt_ja=揖西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isai Village|1=Issai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498437}}. It is designated as an {{ill|Old Hometown Shrine|ja|旧郷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Hometown Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} and a {{ill|Former Sacred Offerings Shrine|ja|旧神饌幣帛料供進指定神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Sacred Offerings Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine enshrines the tutelary deities of the Hase clan, {{ill|Seoitsu‐hime no Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoitsu‐hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13579836}}, and of the Wakisaka clan, {{ill|Tatemina no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9150391}}, in a combined enshrinement. == Enshrined Deities == === Three Main Hall Deities === Three Great Deities of the Shrine * Iseki Daimyōjin – {{ill|Amaterasu Kuniteruhiko Hikari Kushi Tama Hoyosui no Mikoto|ja|天照国照彦火明櫛玉饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Kuniteruhiko Hikari Kushi Tama Hoyosui no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10949054}}{{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|en|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|qid=Q11498726|lt=Harima Fudoki|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}} states that he is considered to be the child of Oho Namuchi ({{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}). : Bestows abundant harvests (via flood control and favorable weather), healing of illnesses (promoting health), national protection (soul pacification, land stabilization, and safety), enduring family fortunes (prosperity of descendants, safe childbirth, and harmonious unions), as well as benefits in aerospace, traffic safety, and iron production. * Yase Daimyōjin – {{ill|Seoitsu‐hime no Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoitsu‐hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13579836}} : Removes disasters (through purification, fortune enhancement, misfortune and evil luck expulsion, sin cleansing, and disease prevention), aids in river flood control (environmental conservation), and protects women (ensuring safe childbirth and child-rearing). * Suwa Daimyōjin – {{ill|Tatemina no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9150391}} : Patron of agriculture, hunting, sustained military success (victory), development (new settlement and industrial promotion), and national peace (prosperity, traffic safety, good fortune, and longevity). === Subordinate Shrine Outside the Main Grounds: Okumiya Shrine === * Okumiya Daimyōjin – {{ill|Takeiwamitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiwamitsuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1502690}} : Grants the fulfillment of heartfelt wishes, protection in martial endeavors (ensuring victory), safe travel, national safeguarding (through mountain management and environmental conservation), and disaster prevention. === Subordinate Shrines Within the Shrine Grounds === Ara Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} : Patron of development, agriculture, and fire prevention (protection against fire disasters). Inari Shrine * {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukano‐mitama|1=Ukanomitama}} : Bestows abundant harvests, prosperous commerce, and household safety. Tenma Shrine * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} : Confers academic success, encourages calligraphy and the arts, and offers protection from lightning. Kotohira Shrine * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}}、{{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Sutoku‐in|1=Emperor Sutoku}} : Grants abundant harvests, traffic safety, protection from water disasters, household security, healing of ailments, severance of harmful ties, and exorcism of vengeful spirits. == History == : According to the shrine legend, in the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Sujin Tennō|1=Emperor Sujin}}[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6318017.html Hyōgo Prefectural Shrine Agency explanation] (which, as explained by sources such as the Hyōgo Prefectural Shrine Agency, is dated 96 BC by mechanically converting the imperial reigns recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} into the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|qid=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} system), the deity {{ill|Amaterasu Kuniteruhiko Hikari Kushi Tama Hayasui no Mikoto|ja|天照国照彦火明櫛玉饒早日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Kuniteruhiko Hikari Kushi Tama Hayasui no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579244|1=Q135579244}} was enshrined at Kameyama in {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}’s {{ill|Ibo District, Hyōgo|en|Ibo District, Hyōgo|qid=Q1042150|lt=Ibo District|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}, and the shrine was established as Amaterasu Shrine. : The shrine legend records: {{quotation| "In the 2nd year of Emperor Sujin, upon the raised platform there shone a brilliant light; in Harima Province too, a similar radiance was visible. In accordance with divine instruction, the Shidō Shōgun {{ill|Tanba Dōshun‐ō|ja|丹波道主王|lt=Tanba Dōshun no Mikoto|lt_ja=丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Dōshun‐ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丹波道主命|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11368587}} of that time commanded, 'Establish a shrine within this sacred boundary,' and thus the shrine was founded." |}}
Furthermore, it is recounted that during a national pilgrimage, the deity {{ill|Takeiwamitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiwamitsuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1502690}}—enshrined as Okumiya Daimyōjin—expressed the wish, "Let my spirit abide here," and caused a large stone to be set up on the mountain peak. This miraculous event was duly reported by Shidō Shōgun {{ill|Tanba Dōshun‐ō|ja|丹波道主王|lt=Tanba Dōshun no Mikoto|lt_ja=丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Dōshun‐ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丹波道主命|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11368587}}, and the stone thereafter served as the sacred body of the shrine. : From that time onward, the deity {{ill|Takeiwamitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiwamitsuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1502690}}, enshrined as Okumiya Daimyōjin, came to be honored as Okumiya Taijin Shrine and was regarded as the guardian deity of the {{ill|Ancient Yamashiro|ja|古代山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Yamashiro|1=ancient mountain fortress in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6431764}} and {{ill|Jōnoyama Castle|ja|城山城 (播磨国)|lt=Jōnoyama Castle|lt_ja=城ノ山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōnoyama Castle|1=Kinoyama Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17211812}}. After the defeat of the {{ill|Wakoku|en|Wakoku|qid=Q10888424|lt=Wa Kingdom|1=Wakoku}} in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|qid=Q608727|lt=Battle of Hakusonoe|1=Battle of Baekgang}} during the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Tenji Tennō|1=Emperor Tenji}} (663 AD), a castle was built in anticipation of invasions from the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|qid=Q9683|lt=Tang Dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} and {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, and the red Akamatsu clan ({{ill|Akamatsu clan|en|Akamatsu clan|qid=Q2659667|lt=Akamatsu Clan|1=Akamatsu clan}}) held the deity in deep reverence. : The timing and circumstances of the shrine’s relocation to Iseki—situated in the area of the present shrine, formerly the site of the original Okumiya near Kameyama—are the subject of various theories and remain uncertain. The present site of Iseki lies behind the oni‐gate of Jōyama Castle and is positioned diagonally opposite to the Jōzen‐ji Temple and the Miyauchi Tenmangū, which were established to ward off evil influences. === Chronology === * In the 2nd year of Emperor Sujin, at Kameyama in Ibo District, Harima Province (the present location of Okumiya), {{ill|Amaterasu Kuniteruhiko Hikari Kushi Tama Hoyosui no Mikoto|ja|天照国照彦火明櫛玉饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Kuniteruhiko Hikari Kushi Tama Hoyosui no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10949054}} was enshrined by imperial command, and Amaterasu Shrine was established; from that time, {{ill|Takeiwamitsuchi no Mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeiwamitsuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1502690}} was also enshrined. * In the era of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Tenmu Tennō|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, the Shidō Shōgun enshrined {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Ushiro Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, which was later known in the late Asuka period as {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Jingū‐ji|1=Jingū-ji}}. * By the 2nd year of Enkyū (1336), during the turmoil of the Nanboku‐chō period, both the shrine and Jingū‐ji were destroyed by fire. * In the 5th year of Jōwa (1349), the shrine buildings at Iseki were rebuilt. * In the 12th year of Eikyō (1440), the Iseki shrine structures were reconstructed. * In the 1st year of Kakyō (1441), both the Iseki and Okumiya shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Kajiki Rebellion, though they were subsequently restored. * In the 1st year of Kōji (1555)The Nakagaitsu Chronicle records this as the 1st year of Tokuji (1306)., the headman of Nakagaitsu village from the Yase clan re-enshrined its tutelary deity, {{ill|Seoitsu‐hime no Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoitsu‐hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13579836}}, by receiving a bunrei from the deity enshrined in Yase of Yamashiro Province{{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}: In present-day Yase, she is enshrined at the subsidiary shrine of Yase Tenmangū and Rokushosho Shrine.. * In the 12th year of Kanbun (1672), when the lord of {{ill|Tatsuno Castle|ja|龍野城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno Castle|1=Tatsuno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10856352}}, {{ill|Wakisaka Yasumasa|ja|脇坂安政|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakisaka Yasumasa|1=Wakisaka Yasumasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612109}}, entered the domain, the guardian deity enshrined at Iida in Shinano Province—formerly worshipped at {{ill|Suwa Shrine|ja|諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine|qq=}}—had its deity, {{ill|Tatemina no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9150391}}, re-enshrined along with the three deities {{ill|Amaterasu Kuniteruhiko Hikari Kushi Tama Hoyosui no Mikoto|ja|天照国照彦火明櫛玉饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Kuniteruhiko Hikari Kushi Tama Hoyosui no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10949054}}, {{ill|Seoitsu‐hime no Mikoto|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoitsu‐hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13579836}}, and {{ill|Tatemina no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatemina no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9150391}}, whereby the shrine came to be known as Iseki Sanjinja. * Throughout the Edo period, the Tatsuno Domain lords, the Kyōgoku clan,After their reassignment to the Marugame Domain in Sanuki Province, the Wakisaka clan, Tatsuno retainers, and the villagers of Nakagaitsu and neighboring settlements contributed numerous offerings to the Iseki shrine. as well as successive generations of the Wakisaka clan, Tatsuno retainers, and the local chō (from Nakagaitsu, Shimizu, Shimizu Shin, Sae, Maechi) and Kuwahara Sō (from Oda, Shōbugai, Shingū, Kō, Tai, Takematsu, Kitayama, Ryūko) communities, held the shrine in deep veneration. * In the 15th year of the Meiji era (1882), it was designated as a local shrine. * In the 45th year of the Meiji era (1912), it was designated as a shrine for offering sacred foods and silk. == Shrine Grounds == * Shrine Banners : Amaterasu Shrine (rear) / Shikichi Iseki Shrine * Shrine Buildings : Main Hall, Worship Hall, Offering Hall, Ema Hall, Purification Font, and Office * {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=Shrine within the precincts|1=Setsumatsusha}} : Ara Shrine, Inari Shrine, Tenma Shrine, and Kotohira Shrine * Shrines Outside the Grounds : Okumiya Shrine : Pilgrim's Lodge File:Iseki05.JPG|Torii Gate File:Iseki01.JPG|Shrine Grounds File:Iseki02.JPG|Main Hall File:Iseki kohjin.JPG|Ara Shrine File:Iseki inari.JPG|Inari Shrine File:Iseki tenjin.JPG|Tenma Shrine File:Iseki konpira02.gif|Kotohira Shrine File:Iseki okumiya01.JPG|Okumiya Shrine Torii Gate File:Iseki okumiya03.JPG|Okumiya Shrine building with the sacred body ({{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|qid=Q11587880|lt=Iwakura|1=Iwakura rock}}) File:Iseki otabi.JPG|Pilgrim's Lodge == Festivals == * 10‐Year Festival Revelation Ceremony A periodic festival held every 10 years, also known as the Revelation Ceremony. It preserves syncretic customs from the Shinto–Buddhist amalgamation period; at the time of the Jingū‐ji establishment during the Hakuhō period (circa 670 AD), the three revered Buddhist statues were unveiled. * Harima's Extraordinary Festival: Sairen Bōzu Held annually on August 14 within the shrine grounds and on the following day at the grounds of {{ill|Ontoku‐ji|ja|恩徳寺 (たつの市)|lt=Ontoku‐ji Temple|lt_ja=恩徳寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontoku‐ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579247|1=Ontoku‐ji Temple}} in Nakagaitsu, this Bon festival commemorates the spirits of the Akamatsu clan who fell in battle during the Kajiki Rebellion at the fall of Jōyama Castle. It is designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901957|lt=Intangible Cultural Property|1=Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Tatsuno City. File:Sairennbouzu04.jpeg File:Sairennbouzu03.jpeg File:Sairennbouzu02.jpeg File:Iseki sairenbouzu.jpeg == Legend of the Water Dispute == According to the shrine legend, {{quotation| "In one summer when a severe drought gripped the land and all the people suffered from a lack of water, the villagers of Motokuni Koshibesō Shinohō (本國越部荘市野保村) ascended this mountain. They breached the embankment of the divine pond to divert water into Karaneko Valley and channel it into Shinohō Village. Yet, as the people dRank the water, both cattle and horses suddenly fell ill and collapsed. Suspicious of this calamity, the villagers consulted an onmyōji. The diviner declared, 'This is divine retribution for having stolen and consumed the sacred water.' Terrified, the villagers dammed the embankment as firmly as if binding a book, and conducted a grand purification ritual before the deities in hopes of atoning for their sin. The residents of Hirai District in Kuwahara Village, witnessing the divine power that enabled the pond’s water to be restrained, came to venerate the deity thereafter known as Iseki Daimyōjin{{ill|Itatehyōzu Shrine|en|Itatehyōzu Shrine|qid=Q11458547|lt=Harima Provincial Shrine|1=Itatehyōzu Shrine}}." |}} File:Kinoike.JPG|Kameyama Mountain top pond (site of a Yayoi period elevated settlement) File:Iseki okumiya05.JPG|Signboard at the water dispute relic site File:Iseki okumiya08.JPG|Left: water channel feeding into Nakagaitsu River; Right: Karaneko Valley == Access == Iseki Main Shrine : Parking: 40 spaces * {{ill|Kishin Line|en|Kishin Line|qid=Q869221|lt=Himashin Line|1=Kishin Line}} {{ill|Hon-Tatsuno Station|en|Hon-Tatsuno Station|qid=Q11520740|lt=Honryūno Station|1=Hon-Tatsuno Station}} – approximately 6 km; about a 10‐minute drive * {{ill|San'yō Main Line|en|San'yō Main Line|qid=Q1193436|lt=Sanyō Main Line|1=San'yō Main Line}} {{ill|Tatsuno Station about a 60‐minute walk == Surroundings == * {{ill|Hyōgo Prefectural Harima Special Needs School|ja|兵庫県立播磨特別支援学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefectural Harima Special Needs School|1=Harima
